text.title: project-closure Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for project-closure as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No formal acceptance — assuming silence means approved.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Closure without written acceptance leaves deliverable status ambiguous; six months later this becomes an invoice dispute with no documentation to support the PM's position.

failure-mode.description: Resources not released — people stuck on "finished" projects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Trapped resources block new work and signal to team members that their time is not valued, increasing turnover risk.

failure-mode.description: Contracts left open — ongoing vendor charges accumulate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Open POs continue to accrue costs and legal liabilities long after work stopped; finance discovers this in quarterly audits, not daily operations.

failure-mode.description: No lessons captured — knowledge walks out the door with the team.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without documented lessons, the next project repeats the same mistakes. Institutional knowledge is the only free input to future project success.

failure-mode.description: No celebration — team feels unappreciated after sustained effort.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Skipping recognition signals that delivery is expected but not valued, the leading cultural factor in team attrition after project completion.
